title: zephyranthes minima subsp. hexandra
intro: Zephyranthes minima subsp. hexandra, also known as the "Small Zephyr Lily," is a perennial plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its white to pale yellow flowers and slender leaves. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Zephyranthes minima subsp. hexandra include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions, including arthritis and bronchitis. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, often in combination with other medicinal plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
